data_notes: Rollups were used as authoritative for last-7 and last-28 training totals (distance, moving time, elevation, training load).

No sleep data were available in the last 7 days (sleep_days_with_data=0), so last-7 sleep/HRV/RHR trend comparisons cannot be made; plan is therefore slightly conservative on intensity and overall volume.
No HRV and no last-7 RHR signals are available; intensity guidance is based on RPE and session structure rather than HR zones.
Readiness status was set from deterministic readiness_and_risk_forecast.json.
Guardrails: enforced ramp rate (max_ramp_pct=10.0%). last7_hours=11.324, allowed_hours=12.456, planned_hours=9.800.

meta.style: Run + bike hybrid with a light deload bias (moderate overreach risk; limited recovery data).

days.purpose: Absorb the recent elevated 7-day load and reduce overreach risk before adding any quality.
days.target_intensity: Full rest from structured training (optional 20–30 min walk only if you feel better after moving).
days.terrain: Flat, soft surfaces if you walk.
days.title: Rest day + easy mobility
days.workout: No training session. Optional: 10–15 min gentle mobility (hips/calves/hamstrings) + 5–10 min easy foam rolling.

days.purpose: Maintain aerobic consistency while stepping down load after a high 7-day load window.
days.target_intensity: Easy conversational (RPE 3–4/10). Keep it smooth; avoid surges.
days.title: Easy aerobic ride (Z2 feel)
days.workout: 75 min easy spin. Optional: 4 × 20 sec high-cadence (100–110 rpm) with 2–3 min easy between (NOT hard; keep RPE ≤5/10).

days.purpose: Add one focused intensity touch while keeping weekly load under control given moderate overreach risk and missing sleep data.
days.target_intensity: Hard but controlled (work reps RPE 7.5–8.5/10); stop a rep early if form/power fades.
days.terrain: Trainer or a steady climb/flat road with minimal interruptions.
days.title: Quality stimulus: bike intervals (controlled)
days.workout: Warm-up 15 min easy. Main set: 5 × 4 min strong/controlled with 3 min very easy between. Cool-down 15 min easy.

days.purpose: Maintain run specificity without stacking intensity and without replicating the high-vert stress seen recently.
days.target_intensity: Easy (RPE 3–4/10). Keep breathing calm; walk steeper pitches.
days.terrain: Non-technical trails; keep vert modest (aim ~200–400 m).
days.title: Easy trail run / hike-run (return-to-run friendly)
days.workout: 70 min easy. If you feel great: add 4 × 15 sec relaxed strides on flat with full recovery walk/jog (keep it snappy, not hard).

days.purpose: Build aerobic volume safely while adding durability work without turning it into a second hard day.
days.target_intensity: Steady aerobic (RPE 4–5/10). No extended threshold efforts.
days.title: Aerobic ride + short strength
days.workout: 90 min steady aerobic ride. After: 20 min strength (bodyweight or light loads): 2–3 rounds of split squats, calf raises, dead bugs, side planks; stop 2 reps before failure.

days.purpose: Long aerobic stimulus with low impact, aligned with your recent bike-heavy load while managing overreach risk.
days.target_intensity: Endurance pace (RPE 3–4/10). Keep it easy enough to fuel steadily.
days.terrain: Rolling; small climbs ok. Keep it smooth rather than punchy.
days.title: Long aerobic ride (endurance)
days.workout: 3:00 easy endurance ride. Fuel practice: start eating within 20–30 min; keep intake consistent throughout.

days.purpose: Second (and final) quality touch of the week, adding running-specific stamina while respecting the moderate overreach risk and keeping total weekly moving time conservative.
days.target_intensity: Controlled steady/tempo (RPE 6.5–7.5/10) but keep it sub-max; if heavy legs, downgrade to easy and skip tempo.
days.terrain: Trail/rolling; aim ~300–500 m vert; avoid very steep hiking grades.
days.title: Steady trail run (moderate) + mobility
days.workout: Warm-up 20 min easy. Then 2 × 15 min steady (comfortably hard) with 5 min easy between. Cool-down easy to 100 min total. After: 10 min mobility.

readiness.rationale: Deterministic readiness: steady (score 56.2). Authoritative forecast sets readiness to steady with moderate overreach risk, driven by elevated recent training load versus your rolling baseline and no usable last-7-day recovery metrics (sleep/HRV/RHR) to confirm freshness.

actions: Because last-7-day sleep data are missing, prioritize consistent bed/wake times and aim to re-establish sleep tracking to better individualize intensity day-to-day.
Post-interval/tempo days: 10–15 min easy cool-down + light calves/hips mobility to keep run mechanics smooth.

Fueling (especially before/after the 3:00 long ride): eat a carb-forward meal 2–3 hours prior; start taking in calories early during the session; include a protein + carb meal within ~60 minutes after.
Hydration: start sessions well-hydrated and add electrolytes if the ride/run is longer or warmer than usual.

risks.message: Moderate overreach risk: recent 7-day training load is elevated vs your rolling baseline; keep this week controlled and avoid adding extra intensity beyond the two planned quality days.
Recovery-data gap: no sleep days recorded in the last 7 days reduces confidence in day-to-day readiness; use RPE conservatively and downgrade quality if you feel flat.
High recent elevation load suggests musculoskeletal fatigue risk on steep trails; keep midweek run vert modest and use the bike for most endurance volume.

snapshot.notes: Last 7 days were high volume with 7 activities and notable elevation (driven by one high-vert TrailRun). Sleep/HRV/RHR are unavailable in the last 7 days, so readiness guidance is driven primarily by load and the provided forecast.
